input_text: I think the last election was about style only insofar as many were tired of Harper's style. Even Conrad Black was moved to describe Harper as governing like a "sadistic Victorian schoolmaster." Many people, including myself, simply wanted Harper out. For the sake of his party and the country he should have stepped down before the 2011 election. Although I disliked the Harper government's anti-evidence bent, including the muzzling of scientists and the cancellation of the long-form census, and I worried that with legislation like C-51 the government was becoming too authoritarian, I didn't disagree with many of its other policy approaches, such as the attempt to clean up the refugee determination system. Trudeau ran as the anti-Harper and it worked. But it only worked because Harper was still around.

Goodie for you.  But you're just one person.  For most of us, traffic has gotten far worse, and water is becoming more scarce as we have millions more people vying for the same (mostly) limited natural resources.  And that's just some of the problems.  

BTW, I've got a vegetable garden too.  It doesn't do squat to help the traffic problems around here.

Oh.....and what does responsibility have to do with the pizza issue (which was being discussed by us)?  Here it is.....Papa John is whining that the NFL's handling of the Anthem protests is hurting his business.  Therefore, he can keep crying out it, or break ties with the NFL.  The "conservative value" way (in quotes because the GOP abandoned personal responsibility long ago....they are also now just as much in the professional victim business as the liberals) would be to make a decision to remedy the situation, not whine and play the victim.

And.....what percentage of the black population is responsible for the 50% of all violent crimes?  I bet it's a pretty small number. That was my point.

I'm done discussing this with you.  But, if you still have a problem with me, then maybe it is you that needs to find a new place...where your views don't get challenged.  I'm not going anywhere.
